meta_title: Roofing in Boca Del Mar, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofing in Boca Del Mar, FL. CertainTeed shingles & West Lake tile. Licensed, A+ BBB. Free estimates. From $87/mo.
h1: Trusted Roofing Replacements & Repairs for Boca Del Mar Homeowners
intro:
Boca Del Mar is one of Palm Beach County's most established residential communities, with tree-lined streets, well-kept homes in neighborhoods like Whisper Walk and Boca Lago, and a strong sense of pride in homeownership. With average home values hovering around $470,000, the investment you've made in your property deserves the highest level of protection from the ground up — and that starts with your roof. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings decades of experience to Boca Del Mar homeowners who need a roofing partner they can trust to do the job right the first time.

Located approximately six miles from the Atlantic coastline, Boca Del Mar sits squarely in a 150-mph wind zone, meaning every roofing system installed here must meet the rigorous standards set forth by the Florida Building Code. While this area falls outside the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), that designation doesn't reduce the threat — South Florida's storm season is relentless, and your roof is the first and most critical line of defense against wind, rain, and debris. Nearly 40% of homes in the Boca Del Mar area were built before 1994, predating the post-Hurricane Andrew code reforms that fundamentally changed how roofs are engineered and installed in this state. If your home falls into that category, the odds are good that your roof is overdue for a thorough evaluation or full replacement.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — The Kings of Service — we specialize in full roof replacements and targeted repairs using industry-leading brands like CertainTeed and West Lake. Whether you're protecting a classic ranch-style home near Boca Del Mar Park or a two-story Mediterranean near Patch Reef Park, we approach every project with the same commitment: superior materials, 100% in-house installation, and the craftsmanship that comes from 60+ combined years of South Florida roofing experience. This isn't a side business for us — roofing in Palm Beach County is what we do, and Boca Del Mar homeowners are exactly who we do it for.

section_why.h2: Why Boca Del Mar Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Roofing Needs
section_why.body:
When it comes to roofing in Boca Del Mar, not all contractors are created equal. Many companies in South Florida rely heavily on subcontractors — meaning the crew that shows up at your home may never have worked together before, may not be familiar with local permit requirements, and may not share the same quality standards as the company you hired. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing does things differently. Every roof we install is handled entirely by our own trained, licensed, and insured in-house crews. No middlemen, no subcontractors, no surprises. When you hire Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you know exactly who is working on your home.

Our team brings over 60 years of combined roofing experience across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. We've seen what South Florida weather does to roofs — from slow seasonal deterioration caused by humidity and UV exposure to sudden, catastrophic damage from tropical storm events. We've completed over 500 homes throughout the region, and our deep familiarity with Palm Beach County permitting requirements, inspection timelines, and wind mitigation protocols means your project moves efficiently from estimate to final inspection without unnecessary delays.

Boca Del Mar's mix of architectural styles — from single-story stucco homes in Boca Lago to the larger estate-style properties in parts of Whisper Walk — requires a roofing contractor who understands how to match the right system to the right structure. A barrel tile roof that works beautifully on a Mediterranean-style home isn't the right solution for every property, and vice versa. Our team walks every customer through the decision-making process with honest, no-pressure guidance based on your home's specific structure, your budget, and your long-term goals.

We hold an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau, and we're fully licensed and insured in the state of Florida. From the moment you contact us for a free estimate to the day your final inspection is signed off, you'll experience the service standard that has made Premier Impact Windows & Roofing the trusted name in South Florida roofing. That reputation doesn't happen by accident — it's built one roof at a time, one homeowner at a time.

section_history.h2: Boca Del Mar's Storm History Proves That Roof Quality Is Never Optional
section_history.body:
Boca Del Mar and the surrounding communities of Palm Beach County have been tested repeatedly by major hurricane events over the past three decades. The most formative of these was Hurricane Andrew in August 1992, a Category 5 storm that reshaped the entire conversation around roofing standards in Florida. While Andrew's most devastating impacts were concentrated further south, the storm's reach extended well into Palm Beach County and exposed a sobering reality: thousands of homes built under pre-1992 codes simply weren't engineered to handle the wind loads South Florida could produce. The aftermath of Andrew directly led to sweeping reforms in the Florida Building Code, reforms that are still being refined and strengthened today.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 brought another hard lesson. A fast-moving storm that crossed South Florida from west to east, Wilma caused widespread roof damage throughout Palm Beach County — not necessarily from catastrophic failure, but from the sustained wind and flying debris that loosened shingles, cracked tiles, and compromised roof decking. Homes in Boca Del Mar with aging roofs or improper installations suffered the most. Then in 2017, Hurricane Irma delivered a prolonged battering that once again reminded homeowners throughout the region that roof maintenance is not a luxury — it's a necessity. Since 1990, the Boca Del Mar area has experienced seven named storm events significant enough to affect local infrastructure and property values.

The pattern is clear: in a market where your home is worth close to half a million dollars, a compromised roof isn't just a repair expense — it's a threat to your entire investment. Proactive replacement with a properly rated, code-compliant roofing system is the smartest financial and safety decision a Boca Del Mar homeowner can make.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code & Palm Beach County Permit Requirements for Boca Del Mar Roofing
section_code.body:
Every roofing project in Boca Del Mar must comply with the Florida Building Code (FBC), which is among the most stringent residential construction standards in the United States. While Boca Del Mar is not classified as part of the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation primarily covering Miami-Dade and Broward counties — it is located in a 150-mph wind zone that carries its own demanding set of requirements. Roofing systems must be designed and installed to withstand these wind speeds, and all products used must carry appropriate Miami-Dade or Florida Product Approval ratings. This is not optional language — it is code-enforced and inspected.

All roofing permits in Boca Del Mar are processed through the Palm Beach County Building Division. Any full roof replacement requires a permit, a licensed contractor to pull that permit, and a final inspection before the project can be officially closed out. This process protects homeowners from unscrupulous operators who might otherwise skip critical installation steps. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we handle the permitting process on your behalf from start to finish — we prepare and submit all documentation, coordinate with the county building division, and schedule the required inspections so you don't have to navigate the bureaucracy alone.

For pre-1994 homes in Boca Del Mar — which make up roughly 40% of the housing stock — a roof replacement also presents an opportunity to bring the structure into full compliance with modern code standards, including enhanced fastening patterns, proper underlayment systems, and updated deck attachment requirements. These upgrades aren't just about code compliance — they directly affect your home's wind resistance rating, your wind mitigation report, and ultimately, your homeowner's insurance premiums. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing ensures every roof we install meets or exceeds current Palm Beach County and FBC standards.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Shingles & West Lake Tile: Premium Roofing Systems Built for Boca Del Mar
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the most respected roofing product lines available in the South Florida market — CertainTeed architectural shingles and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. Both product families are selected specifically because they meet the performance demands of Palm Beach County's wind zone requirements while offering the aesthetic versatility that Boca Del Mar homeowners expect.

CertainTeed's Landmark Series architectural shingles represent the gold standard in dimensional shingle roofing. Engineered for long-term durability in high-humidity, high-wind environments, Landmark Series shingles carry a Class A fire rating and are designed to resist algae growth — a critical feature in South Florida's subtropical climate. The heavy laminate construction provides the dimensional look of wood shake without the maintenance concerns, and the extensive color palette makes it easy to complement Boca Del Mar's diverse range of home styles. CertainTeed's SureStart Plus warranty program provides strong long-term coverage, and Landmark shingles installed with proper sealing strips meet the FBC requirements for Palm Beach County's wind zone.

For homeowners with tile roofs — a defining feature of many Mediterranean, Spanish, and contemporary homes throughout Boca Del Mar, Whisper Walk, and Boca Lago — West Lake concrete and clay tile systems deliver unmatched longevity and curb appeal. Concrete tile is exceptionally durable, fire resistant, and well-suited to Florida's climate, while clay tile offers a traditional aesthetic with extraordinary lifespan potential. West Lake tile systems are manufactured with Florida's weather conditions in mind and installed using hurricane-rated mortar and mechanical fastening systems that comply with current Palm Beach County code requirements. Whether your project calls for a flat concrete tile, a classic barrel profile, or a distinctive S-curve, West Lake's product line covers the full spectrum of tile roofing options.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in Boca Del Mar? Investment, Financing & Long-Term Value
section_cost.body:
For a home valued around $470,000 in Boca Del Mar, a full roof replacement represents one of the highest-value home improvement investments you can make — both in terms of immediate protection and long-term financial return. The cost of a new roofing system varies depending on roof size, pitch, material selection, and the condition of the existing deck and underlayment. CertainTeed Landmark Series shingle replacements typically offer the most accessible entry point, while West Lake tile systems — given the additional labor and structural considerations — represent a larger upfront investment. Every estimate Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides is detailed, transparent, and based on a thorough assessment of your specific home.

We understand that a roofing project is a significant expenditure, which is why Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options designed to make the decision easier. Qualified homeowners can begin their project with $0 down, with monthly payments starting as low as $87 per month. This allows you to protect your home now — before the next storm season arrives — without waiting until you've accumulated the full project cost in savings. Our financing options are straightforward, and our team walks you through every available program so you can choose what fits your budget.

Beyond the installation itself, the financial case for a new roof in Boca Del Mar is compelling. A properly installed, code-compliant roofing system adds measurable resale value to your home — industry data consistently shows that roof replacement delivers strong return on investment in the South Florida real estate market. Buyers in the $400,000–$500,000 price range are increasingly savvy about roof age and condition, and a newly installed system is a powerful selling point. Add in the insurance savings that come with a current wind mitigation report and a newer roof, and the total financial picture becomes even clearer.

section_insurance.h2: How a New Roof in Boca Del Mar Can Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance Premiums
section_insurance.body:
Homeowner's insurance in Palm Beach County has become one of the most significant ongoing expenses for Boca Del Mar residents, and for good reason — South Florida's exposure to hurricane risk means carriers price their policies to reflect that reality. What many homeowners don't fully appreciate, however, is the substantial premium relief available to those who invest in a new, hurricane-rated roofing system. The connection between your roof and your insurance costs is direct, measurable, and worth understanding before your next renewal.

The key mechanism is the wind mitigation inspection, a standardized evaluation that documents your home's resistance to wind-driven damage. After Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs a new CertainTeed shingle or West Lake tile system with proper underlayment, fastening patterns, and secondary water barrier treatments, your home becomes eligible for significantly improved wind mitigation credits. These credits — applied by your insurance carrier after reviewing an inspector's report — can translate into hundreds or even thousands of dollars in annual premium savings, depending on your carrier and current policy structure. Common insurers operating in Palm Beach County have specific discount schedules tied to roof age, roof covering type, and installation quality, all of which are addressed by a Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installation.

For pre-1994 homes in Boca Del Mar, the insurance implications of a new roof are even more significant. Many carriers in Florida now require roof replacement or apply severe surcharges to policies on homes with roofs approaching or exceeding 15–20 years of age. Citizens Property Insurance and numerous private carriers operating in Palm Beach County have tightened their underwriting standards substantially in recent years. A new roof doesn't just qualify you for discounts — in some cases, it keeps your policy from being cancelled altogether. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides documentation to support your wind mitigation inspection, helping you maximize your credits from day one.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Boca Del Mar home needs a full roof replacement or just repairs?
faqs.a: The best starting point is a professional inspection. Homes in Boca Del Mar that were built before 1994 — approximately 40% of the local housing stock — are particularly likely to have roofing systems that no longer meet current code standards, regardless of visible condition. Signs that a full replacement may be warranted include granule loss in gutters, multiple missing or cracked tiles, sagging deck areas, or a roof that is approaching or past the 15–20 year mark. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free, no-obligation inspections to help you make an informed decision.

faqs.q: What does a roof replacement cost in Boca Del Mar, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Roof replacement costs vary based on the size of your home, roof pitch, material choice, and deck condition. For Boca Del Mar homes — which average around $470,000 in value — a CertainTeed architectural shingle system or West Lake tile installation is a significant but high-return investment. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options available to qualified homeowners, so you can move forward with protecting your home without waiting years to save the full amount.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my roof in Boca Del Mar, and does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handle that?
faqs.a: Yes — all full roof replacements in Boca Del Mar require a permit through the Palm Beach County Building Division, and a licensed contractor must pull the permit on your behalf. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permitting process for our customers, from preparing and submitting documentation to scheduling the required inspections. You don't need to navigate the county building department — we handle it so you can focus on the project outcome, not the paperwork.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take for a typical home in Boca Del Mar?
faqs.a: Most standard roof replacements on Boca Del Mar homes are completed within one to three days of active installation, depending on roof size, complexity, and material type. Tile roofs generally require more time than shingle systems due to the additional structural and mortar work involved. The overall project timeline — from signed contract to final county inspection — typically spans two to four weeks, accounting for permitting and inspection scheduling. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing keeps customers informed at every stage so there are no unexpected delays.

faqs.q: Will a new roof reduce my homeowner's insurance premiums in Palm Beach County?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — and sometimes substantially. A new, hurricane-rated roofing system installed by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing positions your Boca Del Mar home for improved wind mitigation credits through the standard Florida inspection process. These credits are applied by your insurance carrier and can result in meaningful annual savings. For older homes transitioning from a pre-code roof to a fully compliant CertainTeed or West Lake system, the combined savings from wind mitigation credits and elimination of age-related surcharges can offset a significant portion of your installation cost over time.

cta_headline: Ready to Protect Your Boca Del Mar Home With a Roof Built to Last?
cta_body: Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for a free, no-obligation roof inspection and estimate — our in-house team is ready to bring The Kings of Service standard to your Boca Del Mar home before the next storm season arrives.
